article_meta.title: video Investment strategist debunks market 'narratives,' shares strategy for upcoming year Charles Schwab managing direc

reasoning: The summary maintains neutral language, avoids adopting Fox Business’s framing (e.g., replaces “debunks” with “critiques”), attributes viewpoints, and balances multiple perspectives. No loaded or sensational phrasing appears in the model’s own voice.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (e.g., “critiques,” “emphasizes,” “references”) rather than loaded terms.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims are either descriptive or attributed to sources referenced in the article (Sonders, experts, judges).
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluations are framed as views of cited actors; no subjective judgments stated as facts by the model.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language; tone is measured.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Does not infer motives or internal states without attribution.
custom_reasoning.slant: Covers unions, government officials, judiciary, economists, and market strategists proportionally.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Minor omissions of some individual items’ specifics (e.g., Hyundai condemnation details) but overall perspectives represented.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Mostly neutral; minor uses like “oversimplified” and “protracted,” but not in an editorial voice.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced portrayal of disputes and policy/legal context without overemphasis on negatives.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats union voices, officials, experts, and judiciary evenly.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Language is clearly more neutral than source’s headline framing (“debunks”).
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Includes all distinct perspectives present in the article with appropriate breadth.
